> > I am Tucker Carlson's interviewee today—we talked about both criticisms and this reform proposal.
And I suspect Elon Musk and his ilk froth at the mouth over 6.
* I appreciate the Signpost giving us a quick review of what Sanger is currently proposing."},{"url":"https://edithistory.substack.com/p/larry-sangers-nine-theses-on-wikipedia","title":"Larry Sanger’s Nine Theses on Wikipedia, Ranked","content":"Plus my notes on his theses.
On September 29 2025, Larry Sanger released his “Nine Theses on Wikipedia.”
If you’re not familiar with Larry Sanger, Wikipedia describes him as “an American Internet project developer and philosopher who co-founded Wikipedia along with Jimmy Wales.” On Twitter, he calls himself a “purveyor of obvious truths, ex-founder of Wikipedia.” His LinkedIn calls him an “Online Knowledge Organizer.”
A lot of his recent press has come from conservative outlets, including Tucker Carlson, the Free Press, the National Review, and Fox News.
I have read and considered his nine theses and would like to offer my own point of view.
Sanger’s third worst thesis: Enable competing articles.
My suspicion here is that Sanger worries he won’t get his way on some of the other theses above, so he’s throwing this one out as a Hail Mary.
This sounds like a proposal to turn Wikipedia into Reddit."},{"url":"https://larrysanger.org/9-adopt-a-legislative-process/","title":"9. Adopt a legislative process. – LarrySanger.org","content":"The Wikimedia Foundation (WMF) has launched initiatives, but these do not establish major editorial policy.
The first eight theses describe some of the insanity.
These nine theses are my Hail Mary proposal to reform Wikipedia.
These things needed to be said.
Still, if Wikipedians were to rally around any of these, one massive problem would remain: There is no legitimate, well-established way to ratify significant reforms.
Underlying Wikipedia’s avoidance of democratic governance, there is one fundamental, structural problem: true democracy requires one person, one vote.
Without a means of legitimizing major changes through an online plebiscite, it is hard to know how Wikipedia might come to adopt any of the nine theses.
Discussion could be launched by grassroots Wikipedians or by the Wikimedia Foundation Board of Trustees.
The primary goal of the convention is to settle on the method and procedure for a Wikipedia editorial assembly."},{"url":"https://wlac.iheart.com/content/2026-06-23-wikipedia-blocks-co-founder-larry-sanger-after-bias-reform-campaign/","title":"Wikipedia Blocks Co-Founder Larry Sanger After Bias Reform Campaign | Talk Radio 98.3 WLAC","content":"Sanger’s recent activities included encouraging more participation from groups he believes are underrepresented in Wikipedia’s editorial process, such as Hindus and American conservatives.
The controversy comes as lawmakers, including the House Oversight Committee, have opened investigations into allegations of bias and manipulation on Wikipedia."},{"url":"https://www.thefp.com/p/i-founded-wikipedia-heres-how-to-fix-it","title":"I Founded Wikipedia. Here’s How to Fix It.","content":"Today, we’re honored to publish one of the site’s founders, Larry Sanger.
I do this, as Martin Luther said when he posted his famous 95 theses, “out of love for the truth and the desire to elucidate it.”"}]}

If it doesn't work, we need to organize an alternative.","score":0.5415791,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://larrysanger.org/9-adopt-a-legislative-process","title":"9. Adopt a legislative process. – LarrySanger.org","content":"The project is governed by an unfair and anonymous oligarchy that likes things just as they are. To establish legitimate and fair governance, the WMF should convene a constitutional convention to create an editorial charter and assembly. This assembly would be empowered to make the sorts of changes proposed in these “Nine Theses.”. I have proposed many other fixes and tried alternative projects over the years, but there are in fact a number of people, including some very influential people, who like things just as they are. All of that said, I am confident that most if not all of these nine theses would be popular among most of Wikipedia’s *readers.* The proposals are a matter of common sense, appealing to common civilizational values and sound rules of editing and publishing, absolutely consistent with a wide variety of political and religious views. Suppose some of the more respected voices in the Wikipedia community were to agree that the project should repeal “Ignore all rules”—how would they implement such a change?","score":0.47943115,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.facebook.com/groups/wikipediaweekly/posts/24619193471035152","title":"Wikipedia co-founder Larry Sanger published his \"Nine ...","content":"Wikipedia co-founder Larry Sanger published his \"Nine Theses\": a long essay that's a mix of critiques of Wikipedia (I assume he's really just talking about","score":0.43755972,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":0.73,"request_id":"ae06cdc7-3685-444e-8681-7ecbda85cf82"}
